description: Construct a right triangle from two leg lengths. The hypotenuse is computed automatically as sqrt(a^2 + b^2). The right angle is always at the base point (vertex A).
utility: Create right triangle from two leg lengths with automatic hypotenuse

arguments.name: a
arguments.description: Length of first leg (extends along x-axis from right angle vertex). Must be positive.

arguments.name: b
arguments.description: Length of second leg (extends perpendicular to first leg). Must be positive.

arguments.name: basePoint
arguments.description: Position for vertex A (the right angle vertex). Must be a point variable or point(G, x, y).

arguments.name: angle
arguments.description: Rotation angle in degrees (rotates entire triangle around basePoint)

alternates.syntax: sss(G, a, b, point(G, x, y))
alternates.description: Right triangle with legs a and b at specified position
Classic Pythagorean triple triangle at point P
Triangle with legs equal to existing line lengths
Create triangle structure without visible outline (for extraction)

examples.scenario: Pythagorean theorem: 4-4 right triangle with labeled sides and right angle
examples.expression: graph_1 = g2d(at(5,30), 13.4, 22, range(-1, 5, 1), range(-1, 5, 1), type(uniform), grid(noaxes)); triangle_1 = sss(graph_1, 4, 4, point(graph_1, 0, 0), t(1)); label_1 = label(graph_1, at(item(triangle_1, type(edge), 1)), "c", buff(0, -1.5), rotation(0)); angle_1 = angle(graph_1, item(triangle_1, type(angle), 1), arcsize(40), type(interior), c(black), t(0.5))

examples.scenario: 3-4 right triangle for second example in Pythagorean theorem
examples.expression: graph_2 = g2d(at(45,31), 17.7, 24.7, range(-1, 5, 1), range(-1, 5, 1), type(uniform), grid(noaxes)); triangle_2 = sss(graph_2, 3, 4, point(graph_2, 0, 0), t(1)); angle_2 = angle(graph_2, item(triangle_2, type(angle), 1), arcsize(40), type(interior), c(black), t(0.5))

label_2 = label(graph_1, at(item(triangle_1, type(edge), 2)), "a", buff(-2, -0.5), rotation(0)); label_4 = label(graph_1, at(item(triangle_1, type(edge), 2)), "hypotynous", buff(-3, -1.5), rotation(30), t(0.5))

examples.scenario: Measure side lengths for Pythagorean calculation
